Scheduled backups no longer stall for hours behind off-site replication. This release rebuilds how a scheduled run replicates to an off-site repo, plus three dashboard fixes.

## Fixed

- **Scheduled backups no longer stall for hours on off-site replication (#95).** A scheduled run used to replicate to your off-site repo once *per container*, inline, right after each backup — so with a blank off-site schedule every one of, say, 44 containers re-opened the remote repo and reloaded its whole index before the next container could even start. Each local backup takes seconds, but a per-item copy against a high-latency backend (Backblaze B2, S3, any rclone remote) took tens of minutes, so the queue stretched to many hours, most containers were never reached that night, and their post-backup updates never ran. Scheduled runs now back up every item first and replicate the whole domain **once**, after the loop — the remote repo is opened and its index loaded a single time per run instead of once per item. What took ~22 hours finishes in minutes. Manual "Back up now" still replicates that one item immediately, and "Back up all selected" now batches its off-site copy the same way.
- **restic's cache is now persistent.** The index/pack cache lived in an ephemeral path and was discarded on every container restart or update, so the first run afterwards re-downloaded the whole off-site index (the "first replication took hours" case). It now lives under `/config` and survives restarts.
- **A slow run can no longer starve the same containers forever.** Scheduled runs now visit never-backed-up and least-recently-backed-up items first (instead of always alphabetically), and an overrunning run no longer spawns a second run overlapping the first over the same repo. Together with the replication fix, every included item is reached every run.
- **Failed post-backup updates are now visible (#95).** When a container's image update can't even be checked (registry rate-limit, auth, network), it is now recorded as a failed "update" run in Run History and the Activity Log instead of vanishing into the server log — so "why wasn't this updated?" is answerable.
- **The dashboard "Errors" tile now clears (#100).** It counted every failure ever recorded and never dropped back to zero. It now reflects only items whose *latest* backup/restore/update failed, so it returns to 0 once everything has since succeeded.
- **VM backup lists are now compact, like containers (#99).** Each VM snapshot row was three lines tall with an always-open restore panel; the restore options now sit behind a "Restore…" toggle, matching the container list, so the list scrolls far less.
- **Dashboard text no longer overflows its cards (#98).** Long labels, names, times and paths now truncate or wrap cleanly inside their cards instead of spilling past the border — the reported stat tiles and the overview "Last result" time, plus a full sweep of every other card that could overflow at narrow widths.
